hypothesis: If training data are scarce or biased across design variables, then tensor completion surrogate models (CPD-S and NeAT) will achieve higher R² in predicting the Young's modulus (E) and mass-normalized Young's modulus (Ẽ) of lattice structures than Gaussian Process, XGBoost, and MLP baselines, because they exploit the underlying low-rank tensor structure and are more robust to non-uniform sampling.
